description_long: This is a weathered stone sculpture mounted on a multi-tiered rectangular pedestal. The front face of the pedestal is inscribed with 'A.D. 1931', indicating its date of creation or installation. The upper section features a detailed relief carving of two human figures, one of which appears to have wings, suggesting an angelic or mythological theme. The stone surface shows signs of aging, with visible discoloration, chips, and erosion, especially along the edges and corners. The base is solid and blocky, providing stability and a sense of permanence. The overall appearance is solemn and commemorative, typical of monuments found in public parks or historical urban spaces.
description: A weathered stone sculpture on a tiered pedestal, featuring a relief of two human figures and an inscription dated 1931. The surface is aged and textured, giving it a solemn, commemorative presence.
description_view_0: Front view showing the inscription 'A.D. 1931' on the pedestal and a partially visible upper section.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the pedestal and the thickness of the sculpture, with no visible inscription or relief.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full relief carving of two human figures, one winged, and the inscription 'A.D. 1931' below.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the pedestal's profile and the edge of the relief, with no inscription visible.
attribute_subject_matter: relief of two human figures, one winged
attribute_location_context: No clear environmental context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a white background.
